_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan: required — each: { command, reason, required, id?, ci_covered? }
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.validation_plan[].id: optional — optional step id; evidence is matched to it via execution_evidence[].step (robust to a command wording diff)
_help.validation_plan[].ci_covered: one of: true | false — optional — step is corroborated by the ci-evidence gate (real CI run), not manual evidence — may be 'skipped'; also auto-recognized when command matches a policy ci_evidence_checks entry
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason?, step? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed' (unless CI-covered); use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.execution_evidence[].step: optional — optional id of the validation_plan step this evidence is for (matches validation_plan[].id)
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.base_sha: optional — pinned merge-base the diff was computed against — makes gate verification deterministic; set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit (computed from base_sha)
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Gate-trust fixes #53 + #54. #53: lock local/CI shape parity — `plumb check` and the CI `run` gate share one shapeCheck; add a parity regression test and write-time discipline (`plumb receipt --write` names required steps lacking execution_evidence) so a receipt-completeness miss is caught pre-push, not in a 25-min CI round-trip. #54: make PASS/REWORK/REVIEW unmistakable via a single verdict-presentation table with distinct check-run name+conclusion, PR-comment title, and annotation, plus a per-verdict published check-run — so a REWORK is never mistaken for a REVIEW (the amos-platform#211 accident).

validation_plan.reason: Runs the full node:test suite, including the new shape-parity (#53) and verdict-presentation (#54) tests, proving check/gate shape parity and the verdict->output mapping.
tsc typechecks the new src/verdict.ts module and its wiring into github.ts/cli.ts — a type error here would mean the bundle is unsound.
Rebuilds the committed dist/index.js action artifact so the CI gate runs the same logic as the source (kept in sync by the dist-check job).

execution_evidence.output_ref: tests 258, pass 258, fail 0 (includes shape-parity.test.ts and verdict-presentation.test.ts)
dist/index.js 258.9kb; grep confirms publishCheckRun + action_required + REWORK title bundled

result_summary: Added src/verdict.ts (single verdict->presentation table) and wired renderComment/renderCiSummary + a new publishCheckRun through it; the CI gate now emits a distinct per-verdict check-run (rework=failure, review=action_required, approve=success). Added write-time evidence discipline to `plumb receipt --write` and two test suites: shape-parity.test.ts (#53 check/gate parity, unit + e2e) and verdict-presentation.test.ts (#54 mapping). templates/workflow.yml now requests checks:write. Verified by the full suite (258 tests pass), a clean tsc build, and a fresh bundle. self_modifying:true because src/github.ts is a protected path. Version tag deliberately NOT moved — release is a separate operator decision.
